title: angelica japonica
intro: Angelica japonica, also known as Japanese angelica or Japanese angelica root, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to East Asia. The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, possessing anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The medicinal properties of Angelica japonica are attributed to its high content of coumarins, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. Angelica japonica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of ailments, including menstrual cramps, headaches, and skin conditions.
